mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 315) triggers expectations of aggressive rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) despite CPIAUCSL > 315.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-expanding GDP-housing regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates labor market overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days via inflationary pressure.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tight-labor regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) despite UNRATE < 4.0% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO < 102.5) signals weakening supply-side capacity, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in supply-contracting periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) despite INDPRO < 102.5.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS > 700k) during low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) creates a positive feedback loop for equity valuations, generating 20d SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in synchronized demand-regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) despite UNRATE < 4.5% and RSAFS > 700k.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 150k) indicates strong labor market dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through corporate revenue growth.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in expanding labor regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) during slowing GDP growth (GDPC1 < 2.0%) signals a monetary tightening cycle, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and GDPC1 < 2.0%.
